# komodo_procedure (Resource)

Manages a Komodo procedure resource.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "komodo_procedure" "example" {
  name = "my-procedure"

  stages = jsonencode([
    {
      name     = "Deploy"
      parallel = false
      executions = [
        {
          execution = {
            type = "DeployStack"
            params = {
              stack = "my-stack"
            }
          }
          enabled = true
        }
      ]
    }
  ])
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The unique name of the procedure. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `failure_alert` (Boolean) Whether to send an alert when the procedure fails.
- `schedule` (String) The schedule expression (cron string or English description).
- `schedule_alert` (Boolean) Whether to send an alert when the procedure runs on schedule.
- `schedule_enabled` (Boolean) Whether the schedule is enabled.
- `schedule_format` (String) The schedule format. One of `Cron` or `English`.
- `schedule_timezone` (String) Timezone for the schedule (IANA TZ identifier, e.g. `America/New_York`).
- `stages` (String) JSON array of procedure stages. Each stage is a `ProcedureStage` object with `name`, `enabled`, and `executions` fields.
- `webhook_enabled` (Boolean) Whether to allow triggering the procedure via webhook.
- `webhook_secret` (String, Sensitive) Override the default webhook secret for this procedure.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The procedure identifier (ObjectId).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import komodo_procedure.example 6627c3e4f1a2b3c4d5e6f7a8
```
